text.title: Design System Drift Dashboard Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Design System Drift Dashboard must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Dashboard / ledger entries are immutable. Edits create a new versioned row with a delta reason.

rationale.source: Append-only data store discipline (Pat Helland, 'Immutability Changes Everything

rule.rationale: Append-only data store discipline (Pat Helland, 'Immutability Changes Everything', 2015).

rule.statement: Every entry has a producer, a consumer, and a decision the entry feeds. Entries without consumers are deleted as vanity metrics.

rationale.source: Anti-vanity-metric practice (Eric Ries, The Lean Startup, 2011)

rule.rationale: Anti-vanity-metric practice (Eric Ries, The Lean Startup, 2011).

rule.statement: Query latency on the ledger is bounded. If the consumer cannot read the entry within X seconds it is a broken instrumentation contract.
rule.rationale: useless data is worse than no data.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Design system v1 to v2 migration (12 weeks) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
